philosophy: Quality businesses, circle of competence, and patience
source: Distilled from public Munger talks and Q&A. All card language below is paraphrase unless a primary-source quotation is added and verified.
_note: Runtime lens. Concentration is justified by business quality and competence, not merely conviction.

master_intro.one_line: Buy a wonderful business at a fair price inside your circle of competence, then allow it to compound.
pillars: Prefer quality at a fair price
Stay inside a real circle of competence
master_intro.why_it_matters: This lens reads unnecessary activity as friction and asks whether quality, competence, and patience justify the position.

strength_intro: One thing you did well through this lens:

position_sizing.rubric_unit: Bet meaningfully when quality and competence align
position_sizing.rule: Concentrate only in the few high-quality businesses you can explain and evaluate; give mediocre ideas no meaningful size.
position_sizing.quote: Act decisively when the odds and your competence genuinely align. (paraphrase)
position_sizing.motive_q: {max_ticker} is {max_pct}% of the portfolio. What demonstrates both exceptional business quality and a real circle of competence?

averaging_down.rubric_unit: Add only to quality at a fairer price
averaging_down.rule: Add only when the business remains exceptional, the moat is intact, and the price is more reasonable; do not average down a mediocre business.
averaging_down.quote: A lower price does not improve the economics of a poor business. (paraphrase)
averaging_down.motive_q: You added to {tickers} while it was losing. What new evidence shows that business quality and the moat remain intact?

exit_discipline.rubric_unit: Do not interrupt compounding without a broken thesis
exit_discipline.rule: Before selling, identify whether the moat or management quality broke; if neither changed, avoid activity for its own sake.
exit_discipline.quote: The large reward often comes from waiting, not repeated trading. (paraphrase)
exit_discipline.motive_q: Of the profitable exits, {winner_early}% later rose. Which businesses lost their moat, and which did you sell merely to realize a gain?

diversification.rubric_unit: Do not dilute quality for cosmetic diversification
diversification.rule: Do not add businesses you do not understand merely to increase ticker count; diversify only when each holding meets the quality bar.
diversification.quote: Excess diversification can replace a few understood businesses with many mediocre ones. (paraphrase)
diversification.motive_q: You hold {n} instruments and {ai_pct}% shares one driver. Which holdings are present only to look diversified rather than because they clear the quality bar?

holding_period.rule: Let holding period follow moat durability, not a changing label; an exceptional business may deserve a very long horizon.
holding_period.quote: A durable compounder should not be interrupted without a business reason. (paraphrase)
holding_period.motive_q: For {incon_tickers}, did the moat assessment change, or did the time horizon change after price moved?

alpha_beta.rubric_unit: Return should come from business compounding
alpha_beta.rule: Separate business compounding from market beta, leverage, and multiple expansion before claiming selection skill.
alpha_beta.quote: Long-run investment return is constrained by the economics of the underlying business. (paraphrase)
alpha_beta.motive_q: You beat the benchmark by {excess}pp with beta {beta}. How much came from business compounding rather than market exposure?

entry_style.rubric_unit: Require quality, competence, and a fair price
entry_style.rule: Before entry, pass three gates: circle of competence, exceptional business quality, and a fair price.
entry_style.quote: Prefer a wonderful business at a fair price to a fair business at a wonderful price. (paraphrase)
entry_style.motive_q: For {entry_ticker}, was the entry grounded in an understood high-quality business at a fair price, or in price excitement outside your competence?
